metrics.narrative: Rachael Coad quantified the environment on the 10 Apr 2026 call: Greencore is a £4bn-turnover business post-Bakkavor acquisition, indirect spend runs ~£650m/year and direct ~£2bn, roughly 30% of indirect spend is tail (2,000 live suppliers, 80% of them in tail and unmanaged), and an £80m synergy target exists that she said 'isn't linked to any solutions at the moment... but gosh, an S2P system would really help.' No quantified savings model has yet been delivered back to her, so the business case itself remains unbuilt.

champion.narrative: Rachael Coad is a developing champion — self-described as 'deeply invested,' 'a big fan,' and expecting to own the solution and run the project. However, she raised a direct, material objection captured in Next_Step__c following the 15 May 2026 on-site: she does not currently have the internal headcount or bandwidth to run a procurement transformation project, which is the stated primary blocker to progression. James Dean (Head of Procurement Excellence) is co-engaged but has no individually attributed quote in the evidence read.

competition.narrative: No suite-level rival is competing for this deal yet (pre-RFP), but Rachael named the existing point-tool landscape directly: Scanmarket for eProcurement, DocuSign as a disconnected contract repository, Click for spend visibility ('works well for directs, not well for indirects'), Pactum for AI-driven autonomous negotiation (in its second year of use), and an unnamed AI contract-redlining tool she 'loves.' A full competitive shortlist is expected to form only once the RFP formally launches, per her own end-2026 timeline.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is extensively and specifically articulated by Rachael Coad on the 10 Apr 2026 call: seven disparate ERPs (13 sites on Infor System 21, others on Great Plains/Dynamics 365, a five-year SAP S/4HANA migration underway), no PO visibility for procurement ('I've never seen a purchase order'), an unmanaged 80% tail spend across 2,000 suppliers with zero risk analysis, contracts historically 'held in a folder on SharePoint' with multiple missed auto-renewals, and a DocuSign repository that 'doesn't tie into anything.' She called the current state 'firefighting' and said 'data will help us fix all of that.'

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified. She referenced only an unnamed senior leadership audience and an unnamed boss ('myself and my boss, we're both of the view...') — neither named nor role-confirmed in any Salesforce field.

decision_process.narrative: Rachael laid out her own sequence on the 10 Apr 2026 call: build the business case first, then go to market with an RFP once she has 'an audience ready to receive it' — explicitly ruling out going to market before the case is built. No formal approval chain or paper process has been named beyond that.

decision_criteria.narrative: Formal decision criteria do not exist yet — Rachael described wanting to see 'the art of the possible' before deciding between a land-and-expand approach or a full source-to-pay rollout, and said requirement-gathering is still to come ahead of an end-2026 RFP. The 15 May 2026 on-site demo was meant to sharpen this, but its content was not captured in Avoma, so any criteria refined there are unread.

engagement.top_event: Three explicit deliverables (UK food references, ECC6 integration plan, Merlin ROI model) unaddressed and due before BRD fit session on 17 June. These are table stakes for Vendor Selected gate.

customer_preference.evidence: Rachael Coad expressed strong personal enthusiasm for Zycus's AI capabilities on the 10 Apr 2026 call ('I'm a big fan'; 'hold on to your seats... I'm ready') but no formal vendor comparison exists yet since the deal is pre-RFP.

competitive_position.summary: Greencore currently runs several disconnected point tools rather than a single S2P suite rival: Scanmarket for eProcurement, an unintegrated DocuSign contract repository, Click for direct-spend visibility, Pactum for AI-driven autonomous negotiation (in its second year of use), and an unnamed AI contract-redlining tool the buyer says she 'loves.' Rachael Coad named all of these directly on the 2026-04-10 discovery call. No formal RFP competitor shortlist exists yet — Salesforce's Competitors__c field is unpopulated, and Rachael Coad stated the RFP will not launch until toward end of 2026, at which point a full competitive field is expected to form. Zycus is presently the only S2P-suite vendor engaged, giving a genuine early-influence position on requirements and business-case shaping.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: This is an early, pre-RFP, single-threaded relationship with genuinely strong pain and buyer enthusiasm but no economic buyer, no budget, and an explicit 'long play' framing from the buyer herself — consistent with a low-to-modest win read and a momentum read that is alive but slow-moving by design.

deal_risk.text: Rachael Coad's stated lack of internal headcount/bandwidth to run a transformation project, amid Greencore's concurrent MBE-programme transformations (supply chain planning, MES, SAP migration), is the primary blocker to near-term progression (Next_Step__c, referencing the 15 May 2026 on-site).

The recorded Close Date (2026-04-12) is 88 days in the past and is a placeholder; the buyer's own timeline points to an RFP toward end of 2026, meaning the real close is likely 6-9 months later than recorded.

win_position.text: Pain is confirmed and quantified in detail (7 ERPs, £650m indirect / £2bn direct spend, 80% unmanaged tail spend, no PO visibility) directly from Rachael Coad on the 10 Apr 2026 call.
No economic buyer is engaged; Rachael Coad said on the 10 Apr 2026 call that 'the business isn't quite as enthusiastic as me yet' and that her job is to build the case to present to it.

The likely champion, Rachael Coad, flagged in Next_Step__c (referencing the 15 May 2026 on-site) that she lacks the internal headcount/bandwidth to run the implementation — a real execution risk on top of the qualification gap.

deal_momentum.text: A genuine two-way buyer email reply was received on 2026-06-02 (~5 weeks ago) and Salesforce LastActivityDate shows further contact on 2026-06-23 (~16 days ago), indicating the relationship is still live.

The buyer explicitly framed this as a 'long play' being 'kept warm' until RFP readiness, with the RFP itself not expected until end of 2026 per Rachael Coad's own 10 Apr 2026 statement — near-term forward motion is intentionally slow.

customer_commitment.text: Rachael Coad has personally driven logistics (confirmed she and James Dean work from the Leeds office on Mondays for workshop scheduling) and described herself as 'deeply invested' in owning the solution (10 Apr 2026 call).
Only two buyer-side contacts (Rachael Coad, James Dean) are logged as opportunity contact roles; no wider stakeholders such as IT, finance, or senior leadership have engaged directly.

Following the Bakkavor acquisition reaching ~¬£4B revenue, Greencore has 'significantly expanded its scale... increased operational complexity and accelerated the need for digital transformation.' Full Zycus AI suite in scope (ANA, Merlin, Merlin Intake) aligned to this MBE program.
• Qualified-stage with recurring April 2026 working sessions and a scheduled 15 May 2026 meeting attended by Greencore stakeholders James Dean and Rachael Coad with Zycus team (Anthony Gray, Shriya Iyer, Parama Sarkar). BD_Engagement is Unengaged in SFDC but the multi-party meeting cadence suggests active discovery underway. The data-driven decision-making framing from the Description aligns naturally with Merlin Insights and ANA positioning.
• Confidence is Medium because the SF Description provides a strong strategic context but the Avoma summary for this opp returned content from an unrelated account (Avoma fuzzy-match error), so we lack verbatim customer-side AI quotes. The score relies on the Description's explicit transformation language and the agentic/Merlin product mix. A confirmed AI champion or RFP issuance would move this to 8+.
